summary: Backend + frontend testing of NEW self-service manage-booking features and settings additions. Backend: 12/12 new pytest tests in test_manage_and_settings.py PASSED - GET /api/public/bookings/{ref} (200 + 404), POST reschedule (200 valid slot / 404 nonexistent / 409 occupied / OK own-current-slot via exclude_ref), POST cancel (200 + slot frees + 400 on already-cancelled + 400 subsequent reschedule + 404 nonexistent), GET/PUT /api/settings persist public_url + notify_reminder, regression: login + shops + Warrington hours (Tue 11:00, Sat 10:00, Mon closed). Frontend: end-to-end booking creation, confirmation shows manage-link -> /booking/{ref}, manage page loads details, reschedule flow (date -> reslot buttons -> success toast -> details update to new date/time with pending status), cancel flow (confirm dialog -> status becomes Cancelled), bad reference /booking/NOPE shows 'Booking Not Found', title = 'Wife To Be | Book Your Appointment', DesignerCredit ('Site Designed and Hosted By Weddings By Mark') present on '/', confirmation, manage page, /admin/login, admin dashboard, and admin settings; NO #emergent-badge on any page tested. Settings page has set-public-url, toggle-notify_reminder, save-settings testids.

minor.issue: get_settings() only inserts default keys if the settings doc is missing entirely. For an existing settings doc from a prior deploy that pre-dates public_url/notify_reminder, those keys will NOT be returned in GET until a PUT sets them. Recommend merging missing default keys into the returned dict (or upserting them once) so brand-new fields show up on GET without requiring a manual PUT.

action_items: OPTIONAL: In get_settings(), backfill missing default keys (public_url, notify_reminder, from_name, etc.) on an existing doc so GET always returns the full schema. Currently a fresh install works; only pre-existing docs from earlier iterations skip these keys until a PUT.
critical_code_review_comments: reminder_loop() uses datetime.now() (local tz) mixed with UTC elsewhere; if the container tz differs from customers' tz, the 23-25h window may drift. Consider standardising on UTC or on Europe/London.

reminder_loop() runs every 30 minutes with a 2h window - a booking could receive two reminders if it lingers >30 min in the window before reminder_sent flips. Actual flip happens in the same iteration so this is fine, but if send_email fails the flag is still set (reminder_sent=True) - a failed send won't retry. Consider only setting reminder_sent when send_email returns True.

public_reschedule and public_cancel are unauthenticated and rely solely on knowing the reference. WTB-XXXXXXXX is 8 base32 chars (~40 bits) - guessable over time. Consider rate limiting or a signed token in the manage link.
manage_link() adds 'View or reschedule' text to emails only if public_url is set; if unset, the email has no link. Consider a fallback message or making it required in settings validation.
seed_data_creation: 6 TEST_ bookings created (WTB-... refs beginning with TEST_Mgr_*/TEST_UI_*). Cleanup fixture cancels them via admin PATCH; one UI booking also cancelled through the manage-page cancel flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two backend test files now: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (iter1, 30 tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_manage_and_settings.py (iter2, 12 tests). pytest.ini uses xdist loadscope 2 workers; run either with '-p xdist -n 0' to disable parallel or default for parallel. Manage-page testids: manage-details, start-reschedule, cancel-my-booking, reschedule-date, reslot-{HH:MM}, new-booking-link. Confirmation manage-link testid = manage-link. Settings testids: set-public-url, toggle-notify_reminder, save-settings. Everything green - only enhancement suggested is backfilling defaults in get_settings().
